inference_steps: The broad bang interval is 11.10s-23.54s, but the tighter grounding places the single metallic bang at 11.50s-12.50s.

The retrieved frame at 12.00s falls inside that tightened bang window and is the closest available frame to the bang in the provided evidence; in that frame, no man is clearly visible well enough to read his posture or action.
Because the bang-anchored frame does not show the man clearly enough to determine whether he is walking, drinking, leaning, or standing, none of the four options is verified.

evidence.evidence_text: A broader candidate interval for the loud metallic bang was localized to 11.10s-23.54s.
A tighter localization for a single loud metallic bang or clang places it at 11.50s-12.50s.
A frame was retrieved at 12.00s, which lies inside the tightened 11.50s-12.50s bang interval and is the closest available retrieved frame to the bang moment in the provided evidence.

In the 12.00s frame, the view is out of the ship's bridge windows toward the sea and deck. No person is clearly visible in the foreground or mid-ground in a way that allows posture to be seen, and no person is seen on the bridge itself in this specific frame.
